svg.text: k-medoids (PAM) — how it works
Step 1 — pick k real points as initial medoids
Unlike k-means, the centre must be an actual data point — never a computed average.
medoid 1
medoid 2
medoid 3
Step 2 — assign every point to its nearest medoid
Uses the precomputed distance matrix — any metric works, including Gower.
Step 3 — try swapping each medoid with every non-medoid
If swapping reduces total distance to medoids, keep the swap. Repeat until no swap helps.
try swap
lower!
Why k-medoids vs k-means
Works with any distance matrix
Gower, Canberra, cosine — all fine. k-means needs Euclidean centroid.
Medoid is an actual client
Interpretable representative — you can profile it directly.
Robust to outliers
Outliers can't drag the centre — they just assign to nearest medoid.
Cost: O(k × n²) per iteration — fine for client datasets up to ~50k rows
